tests.description: chromaticDispersion.glsl file exists and exports reusable GLSL functions
steps: Import chromaticDispersion.glsl as raw string via ?raw import
Assert the imported string is non-empty
Assert the string contains a function named chromaticPoint with signature (vec3, vec2, float)
Assert the string contains a function named chromaticLine with signature (vec3, float)
tests.expected: The GLSL include file exists and contains both chromaticPoint and chromaticLine function definitions
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)

tests.description: chromaticPoint function returns original color when dispersion is 0.0
steps: Read chromaticDispersion.glsl source
Verify the function body contains a guard or early return path for dispersion ≈ 0.0
Check for pattern: when dispersion equals 0.0, the offset magnitude is 0.0, so R/G/B sample at the same position
tests.expected: The chromaticPoint function has a code path where dispersion=0.0 produces zero offset, meaning output equals input color
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)

tests.description: chromaticLine function returns original color when dispersion is 0.0
steps: Read chromaticDispersion.glsl source
Verify chromaticLine function has a guard or multiplicative path where dispersion=0.0 yields no channel modification
Check that warm/cool channel shifts scale proportionally to dispersion
tests.expected: The chromaticLine function produces no channel modification when dispersion=0.0
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)

tests.description: uDispersion uniform is declared in pointCloud system init
steps: Create a pointCloud via createPointCloud()
Call init(scene, seed, defaultParams)
Get the Points mesh from the scene
Access the ShaderMaterial uniforms
Assert uniforms.uDispersion exists
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.0
tests.expected: uDispersion uniform is present with initial value 0.0 in pointCloud's ShaderMaterial
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

steps: Create a particleField system
Call init(scene, seed, defaultParams)
Get the Points mesh from the scene
Access the ShaderMaterial uniforms
Assert uniforms.uDispersion exists with value 0.0
tests.expected: uDispersion uniform is present with initial value 0.0 in particleField's ShaderMaterial
acceptanceCriteria: Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: pointWarp.frag.glsl contains uniform float uDispersion declaration
steps: Import pointWarp.frag.glsl as raw string
Assert the string contains 'uniform float uDispersion'
tests.expected: pointWarp.frag.glsl declares the uDispersion uniform
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: Point-based fragment shaders use chromaticPoint or equivalent per-channel offset logic with gl_PointCoord
steps: Import pointWarp.frag.glsl, particleWarp.frag.glsl, ribbonWarp.frag.glsl, crystalWarp.frag.glsl as raw strings
For each shader, assert it references uDispersion in an expression (not just declared)
Assert each shader contains gl_PointCoord-based offset logic (e.g., vec2 containing offset derived from uDispersion)
Assert each shader produces per-channel (R/G/B) separation using different sample coordinates
tests.expected: All 4 point-based fragment shaders apply chromatic aberration via gl_PointCoord offsets scaled by uDispersion
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: voronoiCell.frag.glsl applies chromatic dispersion to final cellColor, not to Voronoi UV lookup
steps: Import voronoiCell.frag.glsl as raw string
Assert uDispersion is referenced in an expression after the cellColor computation (after mix(edgeColor, vColor, edgeFactor))
Assert uDispersion is NOT referenced before or within the Voronoi grid/cell loop (floor/fract/voronoiHash section)
tests.expected: Chromatic dispersion is applied post-Voronoi computation to avoid artifacts in the cellular pattern
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Color mapping respects the existing palette system

tests.description: Line-based fragment shaders use multiplicative RGB channel shift for chromatic dispersion
steps: Import constellation.frag.glsl, electricArcConstellation.frag.glsl, wireframe.frag.glsl as raw strings
For each shader, assert uDispersion is referenced in an expression after hsl2rgb color computation
Assert the dispersion logic modifies R, G, B channels differently (warm R boost, cool B boost pattern)
Assert the dispersion modification occurs before the fog desaturation block
tests.expected: All 3 line-based fragment shaders apply chromatic dispersion as multiplicative RGB shift after palette color computation and before fog
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: microGeo.frag.glsl applies chromatic dispersion after lighting, before fog
steps: Import microGeo.frag.glsl as raw string
Assert uDispersion is referenced after the lighting computation (vColor * lighting)
Assert the dispersion modification occurs before the fog desaturation block (fogTint)
tests.expected: microGeo fragment shader applies chromatic dispersion between lighting and fog stages
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: dispersion value in VisualParams is computed from combined bass and treble energy
steps: Import mapSignalsToVisuals
Call with bass=0, treble=0 and verify result has dispersion property near 0
Call with bass=255, treble=0 and verify dispersion is moderate (both max(bass,treble) and multiplicative terms contribute)
Call with bass=0, treble=255 and verify dispersion is moderate
Call with bass=255, treble=255 and verify dispersion is near maximum (close to 1.0)
Verify dispersion is always in [0, 1] range
tests.expected: dispersion is near-zero when both bass and treble are low, moderate when one is active, pronounced when both are high
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: dispersion is 0 when both bass and treble are 0
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=0, treble=0
Assert result.dispersion === 0 or result.dispersion is very close to 0 (< 0.001)
tests.expected: Zero audio energy produces zero dispersion — no chromatic effect at silence
acceptanceCriteria: Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: dispersion is higher when both bass and treble are high vs only one being high
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=200, treble=0 -> d1
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=0, treble=200 -> d2
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=200, treble=200 -> d3
Assert d3.dispersion > d1.dispersion
Assert d3.dispersion > d2.dispersion
tests.expected: Combined bass+treble produces more dispersion than either alone (multiplicative term in formula)
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: dispersion is clamped to [0, 1] for extreme input values
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=255, treble=255
Assert dispersion <= 1.0
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=0, treble=0
Assert dispersion >= 0.0
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=300, treble=300 (above normal range)
Assert dispersion <= 1.0
tests.expected: dispersion is always clamped to [0, 1] regardless of input extremes
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: dispersion is scaled by motionAmplitude when prefers-reduced-motion is true
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=200, treble=200, prefersReducedMotion=false -> normalResult
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass=200, treble=200, prefersReducedMotion=true -> reducedResult
Assert reducedResult.dispersion < normalResult.dispersion
Assert reducedResult.dispersion is very small (reduced-motion amplitude is 0.2)
tests.expected: Reduced-motion preference significantly reduces dispersion intensity
acceptanceCriteria: Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: dispersion field is a finite number in VisualParams for all valid inputs
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with various input combinations (null geo, null DPR, zero bass/treble, max bass/treble, edge cases)
For each result, assert typeof dispersion === 'number'
Assert Number.isFinite(dispersion) === true
Assert dispersion is not NaN
tests.expected: dispersion is always a valid finite number regardless of input combination
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: pointCloud draw() updates uDispersion uniform from frame params
steps: Create a pointCloud and init with scene + seed + params
Create a FrameState with params.dispersion = 0.7
Call draw(scene, frame)
Access the ShaderMaterial uniforms
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.7
tests.expected: uDispersion uniform is updated each frame from params.dispersion
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: pointCloud draw() defaults uDispersion to 0 when params.dispersion is undefined
steps: Create a pointCloud and init
Create a FrameState with params that does NOT include dispersion field
Call draw(scene, frame)
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.0
tests.expected: Missing dispersion in params falls back to 0.0 (no chromatic effect)
acceptanceCriteria: Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: constellationLines draw() updates uDispersion uniform from frame params
steps: Create a constellationLines system and init
Create a FrameState with params.dispersion = 0.5
Call draw(scene, frame)
Access the ShaderMaterial uniforms
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.5
tests.expected: Line-based system properly updates uDispersion from frame params
acceptanceCriteria: Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: wireframePolyhedra draw() updates uDispersion uniform from frame params
steps: Create a wireframePolyhedra system and init
Create a FrameState with params.dispersion = 0.8
Call draw(scene, frame)
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.8
tests.expected: wireframePolyhedra properly updates uDispersion from frame params
acceptanceCriteria: Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: microGeometry draw() updates uDispersion uniform from frame params
steps: Create a microGeometry system and init
Create a FrameState with params.dispersion = 0.6
Call draw(scene, frame)
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.6
tests.expected: microGeometry properly updates uDispersion from frame params
acceptanceCriteria: Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: crystalField draw() updates uDispersion uniform from frame params
steps: Create a crystalField system and init
Create a FrameState with params.dispersion = 0.4
Call draw(scene, frame)
Assert uniforms.uDispersion.value === 0.4
tests.expected: crystalField properly updates uDispersion from frame params
acceptanceCriteria: Can be applied to point-based and line-based geometry systems

tests.description: Point-based fragment shaders preserve palette color at dispersion=0 (no offset at center)
steps: Read pointWarp.frag.glsl source
Verify that when uDispersion=0.0, the offset variable evaluates to 0.0
Verify that R, G, B channels all sample from the same (center) position when offset=0
This means the output color equals vColor (no chromatic split)
tests.expected: At dispersion=0, all channels sample identically, preserving the original palette color without any aberration
acceptanceCriteria: Color mapping respects the existing palette system
A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)

tests.description: Line-based fragment shaders preserve palette color at dispersion=0 (multiplicative identity)
steps: Read constellation.frag.glsl, wireframe.frag.glsl, electricArcConstellation.frag.glsl source
For each, verify that when uDispersion=0.0, the R/G/B channel multipliers evaluate to 1.0
This means color.r, color.g, color.b are unchanged at zero dispersion
tests.expected: At dispersion=0, multiplicative channel modifiers are 1.0 (identity), preserving the palette-derived hsl2rgb color
acceptanceCriteria: Color mapping respects the existing palette system

tests.description: Dispersion offset magnitude is bounded (max ~8% of point radius for point-based)
steps: Read chromaticDispersion.glsl or pointWarp.frag.glsl source
Locate the offset computation: offset = uDispersion * <coefficient>
Assert the coefficient is approximately 0.08 (8% of normalized gl_PointCoord radius 0.5)
Verify maximum offset at uDispersion=1.0 is ~0.08
tests.expected: Maximum chromatic offset is bounded to prevent excessive RGB fringing that would overpower the palette
acceptanceCriteria: Color mapping respects the existing palette system
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: Line-based chromatic shift coefficients are bounded (max ~0.3 for R, ~0.25 for B)
steps: Read constellation.frag.glsl or chromaticDispersion.glsl source
Locate the line-based channel shift coefficients
Assert R channel boost coefficient is approximately 0.3
Assert B channel boost coefficient is approximately 0.25
Assert G channel reduction coefficient is approximately 0.1
tests.expected: Channel shift magnitudes are bounded to keep dispersion subtle and palette-respecting
acceptanceCriteria: Color mapping respects the existing palette system
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: dispersion computation uses pow(1.5) curve for subtle-to-pronounced response
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with low bass=50, treble=50 -> lowResult
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with medium bass=128, treble=128 -> midResult
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with high bass=240, treble=240 -> highResult
Assert lowResult.dispersion is very small (< 0.1)
Assert midResult.dispersion is moderate
Assert highResult.dispersion is close to 1.0
Verify the growth is non-linear (super-linear above midpoint)
tests.expected: pow(1.5) curve ensures effect is near-invisible at low energy and only becomes strong at high combined energy
acceptanceCriteria: Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: VisualParams interface includes dispersion field
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with default inputs
Assert result has property 'dispersion'
Assert typeof result.dispersion === 'number'
tests.expected: VisualParams type includes the dispersion field and mapSignalsToVisuals returns it
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: All 9 fragment shaders contain uDispersion in an expression (used, not just declared)
steps: Import all 9 fragment shaders as raw strings: pointWarp, particleWarp, ribbonWarp, crystalWarp, voronoiCell, constellation, electricArcConstellation, wireframe, microGeo
For each shader, use regex to find uDispersion usage beyond the uniform declaration
Assert each shader references uDispersion in at least one expression (e.g., multiplication, function call argument)
tests.expected: All 9 fragment shaders actively use uDispersion in their rendering logic, not just declare it
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)
Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: No fragment shader has mismatched uniform declarations vs TypeScript uniform objects
steps: For each geometry system, get the ShaderMaterial after init()
Extract uniform names from the TypeScript uniforms object
Read the corresponding fragment shader GLSL source
Extract all 'uniform float/vec2/vec3/mat4 uXxx;' declarations from the GLSL
Assert every GLSL-declared uniform has a corresponding entry in the TypeScript uniforms object
tests.expected: No GLSL uniform is declared without a matching TypeScript-side value, preventing WebGL validation errors
acceptanceCriteria: Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: dispersion is pure — same bass/treble inputs produce same dispersion output
steps: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with identical inputs twice
Assert result1.dispersion === result2.dispersion
tests.expected: Dispersion computation is deterministic — same audio inputs yield same value
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: Render loop passes dispersion to geometry system draw() via FrameState params
steps: Create a mock geometry system with a spy on draw()
Create a mock analyser pipeline that returns frequency data with non-zero bass and treble
Start the render loop with full deps (seed, signals, geo, analyser)
Let 2-3 frames execute
Inspect the FrameState passed to geometrySystem.draw()
Assert frame.params has a 'dispersion' property
Assert frame.params.dispersion is a finite number >= 0
tests.expected: The render loop computes dispersion from audio energy and includes it in the FrameState.params passed to all geometry systems
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy

tests.description: Render loop dispersion is near 0 when audio is silent (synthetic bass fallback produces minimal dispersion)
steps: Create a mock geometry system with a spy on draw()
Create a mock analyser pipeline that returns all-zero frequency data (silence)
Start the render loop
Let 2-3 frames execute
Inspect the FrameState.params.dispersion value
Assert dispersion is very small (the synthetic bass fallback of ~30-55 alone should produce very little dispersion since treble is 0)
tests.expected: Silent audio produces near-zero dispersion — the synthetic bass alone does not create visible chromatic effect
acceptanceCriteria: Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy

tests.description: No forbidden storage APIs are accessed during dispersion computation
steps: Spy on localStorage.getItem, localStorage.setItem, document.cookie setter
Call mapSignalsToVisuals with high bass/treble to trigger dispersion computation
Assert none of the storage spies were called
tests.expected: Dispersion computation is purely mathematical with no side effects or storage access
acceptanceCriteria: Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: Build compiles successfully with all chromatic dispersion changes
steps: Run npm run build
Assert exit code is 0
Assert no TypeScript errors in output
tests.expected: Production build succeeds with no type errors from the new dispersion field, uniform declarations, or GLSL imports
acceptanceCriteria: Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL errors

tests.description: Point-based shaders use smooth alpha blending per channel, not hard discard per channel
steps: Read pointWarp.frag.glsl source
Verify the chromatic offset code does NOT use discard for individual R/B channel samples
Verify R and B channels use smoothstep-based alpha blending for soft falloff at offset positions
The only hard discard should be the center-distance check (dist > 0.5)
tests.expected: Per-channel alpha is soft via smoothstep to avoid visible seams where one channel is discarded but another isn't
acceptanceCriteria: A new fragment shader applies per-channel color offset (chromatic aberration)

tests.description: dispersion value increases monotonically with increasing audio energy
steps: Generate a series of inputs where both bass and treble increase from 0 to 255 in steps of 50
Compute dispersion for each step
Assert each successive dispersion value is >= the previous one
tests.expected: Dispersion response is monotonically increasing — more energy always means more (or equal) dispersion
acceptanceCriteria: Dispersion amount is driven by combined bass+treble energy
Effect is subtle at low energy and pronounced at high energy
